en.extract:
The drab whistler (Pachycephala griseonota) is a species of bird in the family Pachycephalidae.
It is found in the Maluku Islands. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.


Taxonomy
The drab whistler was formally described in 1862 as Pachycephala griseonota by the English zoologist George Gray. The specific epiphet is from Medieval Latin griseum meaning "grey". Gray mistakenly believed that his specimen had been collected on the island of Mysol which lies to the west of New Guinea. The locality has been corrected to the larger island of Seram.
Six subspecies are recognised:

P. g. lineolata Wallace, AR, 1863 – Peleng, in Banggai Island, and Taliabu, Seho, and Sanana, in Sula Islands (off eastern Sulawesi)
P. g. cinerascens Salvadori, AT, 1878 – northern Moluccas (Morotai, Halmahera, Ternate, Tidore, and Bacan Islands)
P. g. examinata Hartert, EJO, 1898 – Buru (southern Moluccas)
P. g. griseonota Gray, GR, 1862 – Seram (southern Moluccas)
P. g. kuehni Hartert, EJO, 1898 – Kai Islands (Kai Kecil and Kai Besar)
P. g. johni Hartert, EJO, 1903 – Obi Islands (northern Moluccas)
The subspecies P. g. johni has sometimes been considered as a separate species, the cinnamon-breasted whistler. A molecular phylogenetic study by Serina Brady and collaborators published in 2021 compared nuclear sequences and found that P. g. johni was closely related to P. g. cinerascens. The study did not sample the nominate P. g. griseonota nor the other subspecies.


References

The '''drab whistler''' ('''''Pachycephala griseonota''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pachycephalidae]].
It is found in the [[Maluku Islands]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

==Taxonomy==
The drab whistler was [[species description|formally described]] in 1862 as ''Pachycephala griseonota'' by the English zoologist [[George Robert Gray|George Gray]].<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Gray | first=George Robert | author-link=George Robert Gray | year=1861 | publication-date=1862 | title=Remarks on, and descriptions of, new species of birds lately sent by Mr. A. R. Wallace from Waigiou, Mysol, and Gagie Islands | journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London | volume=3 | pages=427–438 [429] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/28672991 }}</ref> The specific epiphet is from [[Medieval Latin]] ''griseum'' meaning "grey".<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=griseonota | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=griseonota | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=12 December 2025}}</ref> Gray mistakenly believed that his specimen had been collected on the island of [[Misool|Mysol]] which lies to the west of [[New Guinea]]. The [[type locality (biology)|locality]] has been corrected to the larger island of [[Seram Island|Seram]].<ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Paynter | editor-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1986 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=12 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=35 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14482152 }}</ref>

Six [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=avilist>{{ cite web | author=AviList Core Team | date=2025 | title=AviList: The Global Avian Checklist, v2025 | doi=10.2173/avilist.v2025 | doi-access=free | url=http://www.avilist.org/checklist/v2025/ | access-date=12 December 2025 }}</ref>
* ''P. g. lineolata'' [[Alfred Russel Wallace|Wallace, AR]], 1863 – Peleng, in [[Banggai Island]], and Taliabu, Seho, and Sanana, in [[Sula Islands Regency|Sula Islands]] (off eastern [[Sulawesi]])
* ''P. g. cinerascens'' [[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori, AT]], 1878 – northern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]] ([[Morotai Island Regency|Morotai]], [[Halmahera]], [[Ternate]], [[Tidore]], and [[Bacan Islands]])
* ''P. g. examinata'' [[Ernst Hartert|Hartert, EJO]], 1898 – [[Buru]] (southern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]])
* ''P. g. griseonota'' [[George Robert Gray|Gray, GR]], 1862 – [[Seram Island|Seram]] (southern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]])
* ''P. g. kuehni'' Hartert, EJO, 1898 – [[Kai Islands]] (Kai Kecil and Kai Besar)
* ''P. g. johni'' Hartert, EJO, 1903 – [[Obi Islands]] (northern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]])

The subspecies ''P. g. johni'' has sometimes been considered as a separate species, the cinnamon-breasted whistler.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=February 2025 | title=Whiteheads, sittellas, Ploughbill, Australo-Papuan bellbirds, Shriketit, whistlers | work=IOC World Bird List Version 15.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/whistlers/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=11 December 2025 }}</ref> A molecular phylogenetic study by Serina Brady and collaborators published in 2021 compared nuclear sequences and found that ''P. g. johni'' was closely related to ''P. g. cinerascens''. The study did not sample the [[nominate subspecies|nominate]] ''P. g. griseonota'' nor the other subspecies.<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Brady | first1=S.S. | last2=Moyle | first2=R.G. | last3=Joseph | first3=L. | last4=Andersen | first4=M.J. | date=2021 | title=Systematics and biogeography of the whistlers (Aves: Pachycephalidae) inferred from ultraconserved elements and ancestral area reconstruction | journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution | volume=168 | article-number=107379 | doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2021.107379 | pmid=34965464 }}</ref>

zh.extract:
褐啸鹟（学名：Pachycephala griseonota；英文名：Drab Whistler），是啸鹟科啸鹟属的一种，为印度尼西亚的特有种。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
褐啸鹟的平均体重约为23.0克。栖息地包括亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、亚热带或热带的旱林、亚热带或热带的（低地）湿润疏灌丛和耕地。


参考文献
